definition: Current FX vol pricing is inconsistent with the rate divergence extreme: carry-to-vol ratios at 90th+ percentile readings in USD/CHF and USD/JPY while G10 implied vol remains at 7-9%, below the regime threshold. Carry flows are inherently short gamma (selling options to enhance yield), structurally suppressing realized vol through dealer delta-hedging. JGB swaption vol rising while USD/JPY vol declines provides cross-asset confirmation of mispricing.
evidence: Both generalists converge on carry-to-vol compression creating positioning overhang
JGB swaption vol risen while USD/JPY vol declined — cross-asset confirmation one vol market is mispriced
August 2024 precedent validated this divergence as informative
Carry flows structurally suppress FX gamma demand through options selling and dealer delta-hedging
open_questions: How long carry-driven vol suppression can persist before mean-reverting
Whether the vol regime threshold itself shifts over time with changing market microstructure
